title: pavonia rogersii
intro: Pavonia rogersii, also known as the Roxburgh's rose mallow or Roxburgh's pavonia, is a plant species belonging to the hibiscus family and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Pavonia rogersii include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Pavonia rogersii is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
